description.content: Sendcloud's Returns API
Create a standalone return

header.description:
If you are an official [Sendcloud Tech Partner](https://www.sendcloud.com/ecosystem/), send your unique Sendcloud Partner UUID as a request header for the system to recognize you.

The header is not required but if it is set, the system will check it. An unknown or invalid UUID will cause a 400 error.

If you are an official [Sendcloud Tech Partner](https://www.sendcloud.com/ecosystem/), send your unique Sendcloud Partner UUID as a request header for the system to recognize you.

description.content: Retrieve a list of returns which have been created under your API credentials.

query.description:
The cursor query string is used as the pivot value to filter results. If no value is provided, the first page of results will be returned. To get this value, you must encode the offset, reverse and position into a base64 string.

There are 3 possible parameters to encode:
- `o`: Offset
- `r`: Reverse
- `p`: Position
  
For example, `r=1&p=300` encoded as a base64 string would be `cj0xJnA9MzAw`. The query string would then be `cursor=cj0xJnA9MzAw`.
(Required) Excludes all returns before this datetime
(Required) Excludes all returns after this datetime
Search for returns with this parent status
Refers to the number of items per page

response.name: OK

header.description:
If you are an official [Sendcloud Tech Partner](https://www.sendcloud.com/ecosystem/), send your unique Sendcloud Partner UUID as a request header for the system to recognize you.

The header is not required but if it is set, the system will check it. An unknown or invalid UUID will cause a 400 error.

description.content:
The Sendcloud **Returns API** lets you create standalone returns easily and efficiently, so you can seamlessly incorporate returns into your existing workflow, ERP or WMS system. This API can be used in conjunction with other core Sendcloud features or independently.

Contact Support:
 Name: Sendcloud API Support
 Email: contact@sendcloud.com
